CAN: compute persistent homology (Rips, alpha, Čech complexes)
build simplex trees manually with custom filtrations
detect topological features: connected components, loops, voids
CANNOT: handle topological signal processing on complexes (use toponetx)
learn from topology with gradients (use pytorch + differentiable TDA)
prove topological claims formally (use z3 for logical proofs)

rips_circle_topology.detail: Circle: H0=1 infinite component, H1>=1 finite loop bar

persistence_diagram_nonempty.detail: Persistence diagram must have at least one (birth, death) pair

h0_infinite_bar.detail: Connected point cloud: at least 1 H0 bar lives forever

alpha_complex_square.detail: Square point cloud: H0=1 (one connected component)

simplex_tree_manual.detail: Manually built simplex tree with persistence

two_clusters_h0_equals_2.detail: Two separate clusters: H0 >= 2 (at least 2 connected components)

two_nearby_points_one_component.detail: Two close points: 1 infinite H0 bar (merged into one component)

two_identical_points.detail: Two identical points: merged into H0=1 immediately
